text.title: Azure Architecture (Well-Architected + Landing Zones) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for the Azure architecture spec. Each rule maps to a binary check + a Microsoft Learn / CAF source.

rule.statement: Spec MUST declare `iac_engine: avm-bicep` for new deployments. The legacy ESLZ Terraform module is deprecated (August 2026) and MUST NOT be used for greenfield.

rationale.source: Microsoft Learn — AVM docs, ESLZ deprecation notice

rule.rationale: AVM (Azure Verified Modules) + Bicep are Microsoft-supported. ESLZ Terraform has no upgrade path and is in retirement; using it is taking on instant tech-debt.

rule.statement: Identity model MUST be Entra ID + Managed Identity for service-to-service + PIM for privileged human access. Service principals with long-lived secrets are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Network-perimeter security fails when an attacker is already inside; Zero Trust authenticates every request regardless of source. Managed Identity removes secrets to rotate.

rule.statement: An Azure Policy Initiative MUST be assigned at the root or platform MG level enforcing baseline controls (deny-public-IP, allowed-locations, require-encryption).
rule.rationale: Per-resource controls drift; initiative at MG level enforces baseline automatically as new subscriptions / resources arrive.

rule.statement: Management Group hierarchy MUST have at least 3 levels (root → platform/landingzones → workload categories). Flat single-MG topology is forbidden for multi-team orgs.
rule.rationale: Flat MG forces per-resource RBAC + per-resource policy assignment; doesn't scale. The 3-level minimum maps to the CAF reference topology.

rule.statement: BCDR section MUST name an immutable Azure Backup vault + an RTO/RPO target. Mutable-only backup is forbidden for prod workloads.
rule.rationale: Ransomware encrypts mutable backups. Immutable vaults with policy-locked retention survive admin-credential compromise.

rule.statement: If the Applies If preconditions in AGENTS.md do not all hold, OR any Skip If trigger fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and pick a different one — applying the rules here on a mismatched input produces wrong output.
rule.rationale: Methodology selection errors cascade. The cheapest place to abort is before any rule fires.
